top_signals.title: U.S. jet fuel production rises after prices doubled in March
top_signals.summary: Weekly estimates suggest U.S. jet fuel production has increased to record highs in response to elevated jet fuel prices after the Strait of Hormuz closed on February 28. Higher crude oil prices and supply concerns, particularly in Europe and Asia, which previously imported much of their jet fuel supply from the Persian Gulf, have driven up jet fuel prices. Much of the increased U.S. jet fuel production is being exported, as domestic inventories remain above average.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Containers say ‘hold my disruptions’ as ocean rates surge
top_signals.summary:
The Gulf crisis has yet to hit global container traffic, liner operators say, as April volumes surged past year-ago levels.

The post Containers say ‘hold my disruptions’ as ocean rates surge appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: MetaMask debuts Agent Wallet giving AI bots self-custody access to Ethereum
top_signals.summary: Consensys-backed MetaMask is rolling out a non-custodial wallet for AI agents for general availability this summer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Bank Workers Swept Up in Criminal Probe Test Chile Secrecy Rules
top_signals.summary: The June arrests of bank workers accused of ties to Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua criminal organization has reignited debate over Chile’s financial secrecy rules, which are among the strictest in the world.
